raw_consumer_statement:
I am disputing a {$22000.00} charge from Home Depot on my Home Depot-branded Citibank credit card for a kitchen renovation that was not properly completed. I submitted a formal billing dispute and uploaded documentation through Citis online dispute portal, including : A letter from Home Depots legal department dated XX/XX/year>XXXX  acknowledging problems with the installation and offering compensation for inconvenience My formal written refund request dated XX/XX/year>XXXX  A PDF containing photo evidence of poor workmanship ( off-center faucet hole, broken cabinetry, scratched tiles, uneven cabinets ) On XX/XX/year>, I received a denial letter from Citi stating that the charge was upheld because services were provided. This ignores the core dispute : the service was defective, incomplete, and acknowledged by Home Depot as unacceptable. I gave them more than three months to resolve the issue and ultimately declined any further contractor access after repeated failures. 

On XX/XX/year>, I spoke by phone with XXXX from Home Depot Corporate Support from XXXX XXXX  to XXXX XXXX. In that call, I clearly reiterated that I was no longer accepting remediation and was only seeking a refund. Citis denial failed to reflect this fact. In addition, a Citi representative previously confirmed that my email address had been incorrectly entered in their dispute system, and that documentation or follow-up may have been attempted but never reached me. This procedural failure denied me the opportunity to respond or clarify before the dispute was denied. 

I am requesting that the CFPB require Citibank to reopen this dispute, review all submitted evidence, and confirm that the process followed meets the standards of the Fair Credit Billing Act ( FCBA ). The handling of this dispute appears to have been procedurally flawed and substantively unjust.
